title: The space between two selectors
checkLabels.t7: The three prices are in the menu
checkLabels.t11: The heading is still dark red
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. Everything is still green.
hint2.untouched: You only work in styles.css. The HTML stays as it is.
why.not-closed: A closing brace or a semicolon is missing.
hint2.not-closed: Every rule has an opening and a closing curly brace.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.lost-p: There are no longer five paragraphs. You write a rule, you do not delete content.
why.added-class: You added a new class label in the HTML. The task is to reach them WITHOUT one.
hint2.added-class: The only class on the page is menu, on the block. The prices inside need none.
hint3.added-class: The prices are already grouped — they sit inside the menu. CSS can use that as it is.
hint4.added-class: Remove the class attributes you added. Solve it with a rule in styles.css only.
why.menu-broken: The three price paragraphs are no longer inside the menu block. You changed the HTML structure.
why.no-prices: There is no paragraph inside menu. The HTML has been rearranged.
why.prices-not-green: The prices are not green. The check looks at all three.
hint2.prices-not-green: Read the selector right to left: which p, and where it sits.
hint3.prices-not-green: If you wrote only .menu { color: seagreen; }, the color travels down by inheritance — but the p { } rule catches it and overwrites it. An inherited color loses to a rule that points straight at the element.
hint4.prices-not-green: The rule starts with .menu, then a space, then p. Both in one selector, not two.
why.no-intro: There is no paragraph outside the menu and the footer.
why.intro-still-green: The intro sentence is still green. It is not part of the menu.
hint2.intro-still-green: The general p rule still says seagreen. It covers every paragraph on the page.
hint3.intro-still-green: Two rules: a general one for all paragraphs, a more precise one for those in the menu. The precise one wins.
hint4.intro-still-green: Change seagreen to saddlebrown in the p rule. Green stays only in the rule with menu.
why.no-footer: There is no paragraph in the footer.
why.footer-still-green: The address line at the bottom is still green.
why.h1-broken: The heading is no longer dark red. You deleted the h1 rule.
blocks.0.text: The Bean Street menu now sits in its own block. Three price lines, each in its own paragraph.
blocks.2.text: You want the prices green. Prices are paragraphs. So you write the paragraph rule:
blocks.4.text: They are green. So is the intro sentence, and so is the address line at the bottom. The rule is not wrong — you simply have no way to say WHICH paragraphs. A class would work, but that is three new labels for something already grouped: those paragraphs sit inside the menu.
blocks.6.text: Between two selectors, a space is not formatting. It means inside.
blocks.8.text: Read it right to left: every p that has .menu somewhere above it. These are not two selectors side by side. It is one condition — about kinship. The element is picked for where it sits on the page, not for a label somebody attached to it.
blocks.9.text: A class is a name. A space is an address.
blocks.11.text: Descendant means anywhere inside, not one level down. Wrap the prices in another block tomorrow and the rule still catches them. Five levels down — still. That is convenient until it is a surprise: the rule also catches things you never put there.
blocks.13.text: The > sign means direct child only. It gets its own lesson.
blocks.16.text: One space, two different questions. The first is one element with two qualities. The second is two elements, one inside the other. You get no error. You get a page where nothing changed, and you go looking for the cause in the color.
blocks.18.text: The p rule paints everything green. Leave only the three menu prices green. The intro paragraph and the address line become saddlebrown. Do not touch the HTML — no new classes.
